content: YAML parser implementation: greenhouse uses a custom minimal YAML parser (src/config.ts parseYaml) supporting nested objects, string arrays, object arrays, block scalars (|), booleans, numbers. Based on overstory pattern but extends with block scalar support for pr_template multiline strings.

JSONL state persistence: state.ts uses append-only JSONL with dedup-on-read, advisory file locking (exclusive create + 30s stale detection), and atomic temp-file-then-rename writes. Same pattern as seeds/mulch.
Daily budget derived from state: budget.ts counts RunState entries where dispatchedAt date matches today (YYYY-MM-DD prefix). No separate budget file; state.jsonl is the single source of truth.

Infrastructure file set for greenhouse: README.md (install, quickstart, CLI reference, design principles, os-eco links), CHANGELOG.md (Keep a Changelog format), CONTRIBUTING.md (dev setup, quality gates, code style, testing, versioning), LICENSE (MIT 2026 Jaymin West), CODEOWNERS (* @jayminwest), SECURITY.md (report to jayminwest@gmail.com 48h response), .github/workflows/ci.yml (exact SPEC.md match), .github/workflows/publish.yml (package.json path trigger, bunx npm publish, auto-tag, gh release), scripts/version-bump.ts (updates package.json + src/cli.ts VERSION constant), .claude/commands/release.md (quality gates, bump, changelog, commit, push)

All pipeline module functions (pollIssues, ingestIssue, dispatchRun, checkRunStatus, shipRun) accept an optional ExecFn parameter for subprocess injection, enabling unit tests without real CLI calls. defaultExec uses Bun.spawn.

Label mapping from GH labels to seeds fields: priority:P0-P4 -> 0-4, type:bug/feature/task -> seeds type, area:X -> '[X] ' description prefix, difficulty:X -> ' (X)' suffix. Unmapped labels preserved in description. biome.json for worktrees inside .overstory must use includes:['src/**'] not ['**'] to avoid excluding itself.
state.ts appendFile/writeFile require parent directory to exist — use mkdir({recursive:true}) on the dirname of the state path before writing. appendRun and writeAllRuns both need this guard.

When merging foundation and pipeline branches with divergent APIs, adopt the test-expected signature as the canonical one and update all call sites. Aliases (readState = readAllRuns) and additional standalone functions (computeBudget, budgetExhausted, getDailyBudget) are the right way to satisfy both test suites without removing existing functionality.

Greenfield build decomposition: 4 parallel leads (foundation, pipeline, CLI, infra) with non-overlapping file areas. Merge order: foundation first (base types), then others. Auto-resolve merges may require fixup leads when parallel leads create overlapping files like types.ts.

pid.ts module pattern: pidFilePath(projectRoot?) returns .greenhouse/daemon.pid path; writePid/readPid use Bun.file; isProcessAlive uses process.kill(pid,0); removePid swallows ENOENT. start.ts detach: spawn child sans --detach flag with detached:true + log fd redirect; stop.ts: SIGTERM then poll 250ms intervals up to 10s before error.

CLI command pattern for runs.ts: export testable pure functions (listRuns, findRunsByIssueId, cancelRun, retryRun) alongside Commander action registration. Actions use program.opts().json for global --json flag and setJsonMode() before any output. For retry, load config to find repoConfig and call dispatchRun; fall back to pending-only reset if config unavailable.

ingest command URL parsing: parseGhIssueUrl() uses regex /github\.com\/([^/]+)\/([^/]+)\/issues\/(\d+)/ to extract owner/repo/number from a full GitHub issue URL. Falls back to synthetic RepoConfig (project_root=cwd) when the repo isn't in config.yaml.

grhs ship command pattern: load config via loadConfig(join(cwd, opts.config)), read all runs with readAllRuns(cwd), find by seedsId, validate status is running/shipping, find matching repo by owner/repo string, call shipRun(), then updateRun() to persist shipped state. Use output.ts (setJsonMode, isJsonMode, outputJson, printSuccess, printError, printInfo) for consistent UX.

grhs status reads .greenhouse/daemon.pid (plain int or JSON {pid,startedAt}), checks liveness with process.kill(pid,0), loads config via loadConfig(), iterates config.repos to aggregate runs from readAllRuns/getActiveRuns, computes budget with computeBudget(). --json flag outputs structured JSON. grhs logs reads NDJSON from .greenhouse/daemon.log, supports --since <dur> (1h/30m/90s), TTY-coloured or raw passthrough, --follow via 500ms poll loop.

name: CLI command batch parallelization
description: 12 issues across 4 parallel leads (daemon-lifecycle, status-info, poll, run-mgmt) with non-overlapping file areas. Each lead spawned 1-3 builders. All 7 builder branches merged cleanly in ~12 min. Key: cli.ts routing was pre-wired as stubs, so builders only modified their command file. No shared module modifications needed.

name: ship-stage-merge-branch
description: Ship stage merge branch pattern: greenhouse creates its own branch (greenhouse/<seeds-id>) before dispatching overstory, passes --base-branch to ov sling, then merges agent work into it via 'ov merge --branch <agent-branch> --into <mergeBranch>' after completion. This avoids the race where overstory cleans up the agent worktree branch before greenhouse can push it. RunState.mergeBranch stores the greenhouse-controlled branch; shipper falls back to RunState.branch for backwards compat.

content: Dispatcher creates merge branch before ov sling: dispatcher.ts calls 'git branch greenhouse/<seedsId> HEAD' then passes '--base-branch greenhouse/<seedsId>' to ov sling. The DispatchResult includes mergeBranch alongside the agent branch. Daemon stores mergeBranch in RunState and uses it for shipping.

Dispatcher default capability is 'coordinator', not 'lead': dispatchRun() in src/dispatcher.ts defaults to '--capability coordinator' via DispatchOptions. Config default also coordinator. Full swarm flow: greenhouse → coordinator → leads → builders.

Dispatcher structured message: buildDispatchMessage(seedsId, mergeBranch, DispatchContext) generates a coordinator spec with task ID, title, GitHub issue number/repo, labels, issue body, base branch, and explicit instructions (decompose→coordinate→merge→close seeds→clean worktrees). Passed via --spec to ov sling when DispatchOptions.context provided.

description: isIngested() excluded failed runs (status \!== 'failed'), causing double-dispatch: same GH issue re-ingested as new seeds task on each poll cycle after failure.
resolution: isIngested() now returns true for ALL existing runs; add getFailedRetryableRuns() + retry phase in runPollCycle() that re-dispatches with existing seedsId instead of creating new seeds task.

content: Daemon retry pattern: to retry failed runs, use getFailedRetryableRuns() to find runs with status=failed && retryable=true, then call dispatchRun() with existing seedsId (skip ingestIssue). isIngested() must return true for all runs including failed to prevent new ingest. Retry phase runs between monitorActiveRuns and new-issue dispatch in runPollCycle.

name: coordinator-lifecycle-parallel-leads
description: Coordinator lifecycle rewrite: 3 parallel leads (dispatch, monitor, daemon) with non-overlapping file areas completed all 6 issues in ~11 minutes. dispatch lead owns dispatcher.ts+config.ts+types.ts, monitor lead owns monitor.ts+teardown.ts, daemon lead owns daemon.ts+state.ts. Auto-resolve handled teardown.ts conflict. Always ov worktree clean before running lint to avoid biome.json conflicts.

content: Dispatcher uses ov coordinator send (not ov sling) for dispatch. Flow: ensureCoordinator (start if needed) → createMergeBranch → ov coordinator send --body <spec> --subject 'Objective: <title>' --json. Returns {agentName, mergeBranch, mailId}. Monitor checks sd show for issue closure + ov coordinator status --json for coordinator health (running field, not alive).

output.ts mode flags pattern: _quiet/_verbose/_timing are module-level booleans with setters/getters; print functions guard with 'if (_json || _quiet) return'; printDebug writes to stderr only when _verbose; printError is never suppressed

description: teardownCoordinator() called 'ov coordinator cleanup <agentName>' which does not stop the coordinator — coordinator kept running after PR ship, picking up other seeds issues unsupervised.
resolution: Use 'ov coordinator stop' (no agent arg; project-scoped by cwd). Prefix agentName param with _ since it is now unused.

description: teardownCoordinator() did not clean up orphaned overstory sub-agent tmux sessions after coordinator stop — 4 sessions (builder-*, lead-*) remained after PR ship.
resolution: After 'ov coordinator stop', list tmux sessions, filter those starting with 'overstory-', and kill-session each. Best-effort (catch ignored). Sessions are named 'overstory-<agentname>'.

content: session-branch.txt lifecycle: dispatcher writes .overstory/session-branch.txt (join(project_root, '.overstory', 'session-branch.txt')) with the merge branch name after git checkout of the merge branch; teardownCoordinator deletes it with unlink() and runs git checkout main. This ensures ov merge resolves to greenhouse/<seedsId> via the session-branch tier without needing --into flag.

buildDispatchMessage includes stop instruction (item 6) telling coordinator not to pick up additional work after closing the seeds issue: 'Stop after completing this task and closing the seeds issue. Do NOT pick up additional work. Your session is scoped to this single task.'

Monitor health check uses 'ov status --json' (not 'ov coordinator status --json'): filter agents by taskId and exclude capability=coordinator. The coordinator is long-lived and always running, so it must be excluded from task completion detection. Failed run = no task-specific agents found + seeds issue still open.
dispatcher.test.ts teardownCoordinator tests must assert ['ov', 'coordinator', 'stop'] (no agent arg) — not 'cleanup <agentName>'. Implementation uses project-scoped ov coordinator stop.

name: dogfood-fixes-batch
description: dogfood-fixes batch: 6 bugs from first dogfood run, decomposed into 3 parallel leads (dispatcher, teardown, monitor) + 1 fixup lead for merge conflict. Key fixes: (1) dispatcher writes .overstory/session-branch.txt so ov merge targets greenhouse branch, (2) teardown calls 'ov coordinator stop' not 'cleanup', (3) teardown kills orphaned tmux sessions, (4) dispatch message instructs coordinator to stop after task, (5) monitor filters agents by taskId, ignores coordinator.
Biome lint fails when worktrees exist because each worktree has its own biome.json detected as nested root configs.
resolution: Run 'ov worktree clean --completed' before running lint from project root.

content: output.ts duration utilities: formatDuration(ms) returns human-friendly string ('Xs', 'Xm Ys', 'Xh Ym'); computeStageDurations(run) computes ingestMs/agentMs/shippingMs/totalMs from RunState timestamps; compactDuration(run) returns one-liner string ('running Xm' for active, 'Xm' for complete). All exported from src/output.ts.

Daemon structured log events: all state transitions in daemon.ts now emit an 'event' field (run.ingested, run.dispatched, run.completed, run.shipped, run.failed, run.timeout, run.shipping_failed, run.retry_dispatched, run.retry_failed) and duration_ms (time spent in previous state).

output.ts duration utilities: formatDuration(ms) returns human-friendly string ('Xs', 'Xm Ys', 'Xh Ym'); computeStageDurations(run, now?) computes per-stage ms from RunState timestamps; compactDuration(run) returns short status string for one-line display. printRunOneLine appends compact duration; printRunFull shows per-stage breakdown section.

Daemon structured log events: all state transitions in daemon.ts now emit an 'event' field (run.ingested, run.dispatched, run.completed, run.shipped, run.failed) and duration_ms in log() calls for machine-parseable structured logging.

Pre-ship validation pattern: shipRun() runs 'git diff --quiet main...branch' before git push. Exit code 0 means NO diff (branch identical to main) → throw error. Exit code 1 means diffs exist (branch has commits ahead) → proceed. This guards against empty PRs when coordinator closes seeds issue before merging agent work.

Auto-merge pattern: config.shipping.auto_merge (optional bool) triggers 'gh pr merge <prNumber> --repo owner/repo --auto --squash' after PR creation in shipRun(). Uses prNumber extracted from gh pr create stdout URL. Does not fail the ship if auto-merge call fails (caller decides).

shipRun() pre-ship validation: before pushing, runs 'git diff --quiet main...${branch}' to verify merge branch has commits ahead of main. If identical (exitCode 0), throws error 'Merge branch has no commits ahead of main — agent work was not merged'. advanceShipping catches and marks as failed+retryable.

Auto-merge config: shipping.auto_merge (boolean, default false) in DaemonConfig. When true, shipRun() calls 'gh pr merge --auto --squash <prUrl>' after PR creation. Failure is logged as warning, not thrown — the PR still exists.

name: shipping-batch-single-lead
description: shipping-improvements batch: 2 related issues (pre-ship validation + auto-merge) handled by single lead with single builder since both touch src/shipper.ts. Completed in ~5 minutes. Efficient for tightly-coupled changes in the same file area.

content: cleanupAfterShip() pattern in shipper.ts: after shipRun() succeeds, call cleanupAfterShip(run, repo, exec) to restore repo to clean state. Steps: git checkout main, git branch -D <mergeBranch|greenhouse/<seedsId>>, git pull origin main, ov worktree clean --completed, rm .greenhouse/<seedsId>-spec.md. All steps are best-effort (try/catch each, ignore non-zero exit codes). Called from advanceShipping() in daemon.ts after updateRun(shipped) succeeds, wrapped in its own try/catch with a non-fatal warn log.
cleanupAfterShip() in src/shipper.ts restores repo to clean state after shipping (checkout main, delete merge branch). Called from advanceShipping in daemon.ts after successful ship.

recoverAgentBranches() pattern in shipper.ts: when pre-ship validation finds empty greenhouse merge branch, lists local branches matching 'overstory/*/greenhouse-<seedsId>', checks out the merge branch, and merges each with --no-ff. Aborts failed merges and continues. Returns count of successful merges. Two-phase validation: attempt recovery, then re-check diff before failing.

Remote branch cleanup in shipper: (1) gh pr merge uses --delete-branch flag alongside --auto --squash so GitHub auto-deletes the head branch on merge. (2) cleanupAfterShip() runs 'git push origin --delete <branchToDelete>' as a best-effort step (try/catch) between local branch delete and git pull.

cleanupAfterFailure() pattern in src/cleanup.ts: after a run fails, call cleanupAfterFailure(run, repo, error, retryable, exec) to: (1) git checkout main, (2) git branch -D local merge branch (preserve remote for forensics), (3) ov worktree clean --completed, (4) rm spec file, (5) gh issue comment with failure reason + retryable/terminal label. All steps best-effort. Called from monitorActiveRuns() in daemon.ts after timeout and agent-failure paths, analogous to cleanupAfterShip() for success.

runDaemon() writes .greenhouse/daemon.pid (via writePid from pid.ts) at startup and removes it in a finally block, ensuring grhs status can detect the foreground daemon. In --detach mode the parent also writes the child PID, but both writes contain the same PID so there is no conflict.

Retry cap pattern: runPollCycle() accepts an optional retryAttempts Map<string,number> (keyed by seedsId). Each cycle the counter is incremented before dispatch; when it exceeds MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS (3) the run is marked retryable:false. The map persists across poll cycles within a single daemon session (passed from runDaemon). A fresh map on restart allows retries again.

name: ready-issues-batch
description: 5 issues across 3 parallel leads (lead-shipper: shipper.ts, lead-daemon: daemon.ts+cleanup.ts+status.ts, lead-cleanup: operational git/GH cleanup). Shipper lead handled 2 issues in same file area. Daemon lead had 2 sequential builders touching daemon.ts in different sections - clean merge. Cleanup lead was operational-only and escalated file/remote-branch deletion to coordinator (hook-blocked). Total ~14min.

content: Supervisor prompt managed via Canopy: 'cn render supervisor' returns the full supervisor agent prompt with 9 sections (identity, run-context, watch-protocol, intervention-rules, ship-protocol, cleanup-protocol, state-reporting, tools, execution-flow). Template variables ({{seeds_id}}, {{merge_branch}}, etc.) are placeholders for per-run injection at invocation time.

supervisor.ts spawn pattern: supervisorSessionName(seedsId) returns 'greenhouse-supervisor-<seedsId>'. spawnSupervisor() builds bash-wrapped command with nesting guards (unset CLAUDECODE CLAUDE_CODE_SSE_PORT CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT), --append-system-prompt "$(cat '<specPath>')" for spec injection, then creates tmux session at repo.project_root. Waits for TUI readiness (❯ + bypass permissions) before sending beacon. All tmux/pgrep calls go through ExecFn. process.kill used directly for signal sending (consistent with overstory tmux.ts).

Daemon supervisor wiring pattern: after dispatchRun(), call spawnSupervisor({seedsId, mergeBranch, repo, config}, exec) and store sessionName in RunState.supervisorSessionName. Monitor loop uses isSupervisorAlive(sessionName, exec); when dead, read latest state from readAllRuns() — if not shipped/failed, mark failed with retryable:false. Removes monitorActiveRuns(), advanceShipping(), and retry logic from daemon.

Supervisor timeout pattern: monitorSupervisors() checks elapsed time (supervisorSpawnedAt ?? dispatchedAt) against config.dispatch.run_timeout_minutes. If exceeded, calls killSupervisor(sessionName, exec) and updateRun with status=failed, retryable=true. RunState.supervisorSpawnedAt is recorded at dispatch time alongside dispatchedAt.

Deprecation pattern: when removing modules from TypeScript codebase, also remove (1) the test files, (2) all imports from other modules, (3) the types only used by those modules (e.g. ShipResult removed from types.ts when shipper.ts deleted), (4) CLI command registrations in cli.ts, (5) test blocks in related test files that test the removed module.

name: supervisor-build-batch
description: Supervisor agent build across 3 phases (5 issues, 5 leads). Phase 1: 2 parallel leads (supervisor.ts tmux spawner + canopy prompt) — independent file areas. Phase 2: 1 lead (wire supervisor into daemon.ts, replacing mechanical monitor/shipper/retry). Phase 3: 2 parallel leads (timeout failsafe on daemon.ts + deprecate old pipeline modules). Both phase 3 leads touched types.ts but on non-overlapping changes (add field vs remove type) — merged cleanly in sequence. Total: ~1800 lines changed, 5 clean merges, 0 conflicts. ov merge --into main was needed due to stale session-branch.txt from a previous greenhouse run.

content: V1_DONE.md audit-driven update pattern: lead-audit scouts gather codebase findings (architecture changes, doc/code mismatches, test gaps, code quality notes, open questions), then transmit via 3-part mail to builder. Builder concatenates parts in order to form complete file. File was untracked in git — created fresh in worktree.

followLog() stack overflow fix: recursive async poll() calls cause stack overflow in long-running --follow sessions. Pattern: always convert recursive async polling to a while(true) loop with await new Promise(setTimeout). See src/commands/logs.ts.

BudgetTracker singleton pattern in daemon: construct BudgetTracker once in runDaemon() before the while loop, pass as optional param to runPollCycle(budget?: BudgetTracker). Inside, use tracker = budget ?? new BudgetTracker(cap). This persists the in-memory counter across poll cycles and handles midnight reset via maybeReset().
